# Social Media and Creator Economy

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


## Domain summary

Manufactured traction appears as clipping swarms, trend simulation, creator farms, engagement pods, comment-section narrative capture, impersonation, and unoriginal content farms.

## Signals commonly targeted

- views
- comments
- shares
- followers
- watch time
- perceived cultural momentum

## Why this domain matters

This domain matters because its proxy signals are routinely treated as evidence of quality, popularity, safety, usefulness, or legitimacy. When those proxy signals are corrupted, downstream systems allocate attention and money incorrectly.

## Typical ethical boundary

The boundary is crossed when activity hides material context, makes paid activity look independent, fabricates engagement, manipulates ranking signals, or encourages audiences to infer real demand from distorted evidence.

## Examples to study

- Geese / Chaotic Good trend simulation
- Business Insider clipping economy
- Phia creator-farm commentary
- Meta original-content enforcement

## Source notes

- **WIRED — The Fanfare Around the Band Geese Actually Was a Psyop** (News / cultural reporting). Reports on Chaotic Good Projects, Geese, networks of social pages, TikTok recommendation-seeding, burner accounts/comments, and the phrase “trend simulation”. URL: https://www.wired.com/story/geese-chaotic-good-marketing-industry-plant/
- **TechCrunch — Everything we like is a psyop** (News / analysis). Includes quoted explanation of posting enough volume across enough accounts to simulate a song trending; describes Phia creator-farm strategy and volume of creator videos. URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/16/everything-we-like-is-a-psyop/
- **Business Insider — Elite clippers are earning big paychecks** (News / business reporting). Reports retainers/performance payouts, $1–$4 per 1,000 views, clipper marketplaces and Discord/Whop/Genni/Vyro ecosystems. URL: https://www.businessinsider.com/clipping-creators-arrived-discord-money-earning-big-2026-3
- **Business Insider — Clipping makes me wonder what is real and what is not** (News / business reporting). Frames clipping as paid/incentivized short-form snippets that blur organic content and marketing. URL: https://www.businessinsider.com/clipping-social-media-tiktok-viral-band-geese-advertising-authentic-fake-2026-4
- **Meta — Rewarding Original Creators on Facebook (2026)** (Platform policy / primary). Facebook is deprioritizing unoriginal content, clarifying original-content guidelines, and says it removed more than 20 million accounts impersonating large creators in 2025. URL: https://about.fb.com/news/2026/03/rewarding-original-creators-on-facebook/
- **YouTube Fake Engagement Policy** (Platform policy / primary). YouTube does not allow artificial increases in views, likes, comments, or other metrics; actions by hired promoters can affect the channel. URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/3399767?hl=en-GB
- **TikTok Community Guidelines — Integrity and Authenticity** (Platform policy / primary). Bans fake engagement, trade/marketing of engagement-inflation services, covert influence operations, bulk account operation, manipulation of engagement signals, fake personas and spam. URL: https://www.tiktok.com/community-guidelines/en/integrity-authenticity/
- **X Authenticity / platform manipulation policy** (Platform policy / primary). Bans inauthentic accounts, fake personas, engagement exchanges, compensated metric inflation, follow churn and automated aggressive engagement. URL: https://help.x.com/ro/rules-and-policies/platform-manipulation
